evals.name: init-memory-structure-correctness
evals.prompt:
I'm building a Django REST API backend for a food delivery app. Multiple restaurants, customers can browse menus, place orders, track delivery. Tech stack: Python/Django, PostgreSQL, Redis for caching, deployed on AWS.

<session_start_context>
routing_hint: explore
docs_exists: false
</session_start_context>
evals.expected_output: PM should: (1) collect project info via wave questions OR directly proceed to create docs/ from the info already given, (2) create the correct file structure: docs/project.md [PERMANENT] + docs/state.md [SESSION] + docs/rules/ directory with constitution.md + coding-standards.md + git-workflow.md + testing-strategy.md, (3) apply Web Application heuristics from docs-heuristics.md (user flows, API contracts, test pyramid), (4) project.md must capture: project type=web app, tech stack=Django+PostgreSQL+Redis+AWS, domain=food delivery, (5) lifespan labels must be correct [PERMANENT] for project.md, [SESSION] for state.md

assertions.name: creates_project_md_with_correct_info
assertions.description: PM should produce docs/project.md content with: project type (web app), tech stack (Django/PostgreSQL/Redis/AWS), domain (food delivery)

assertions.name: creates_state_md_with_correct_phase
assertions.description: PM should produce docs/state.md with Phase: CLARIFY and Action: discuss or explore

assertions.name: creates_rules_directory
assertions.description: PM should create docs/rules/ with at least constitution.md and coding-standards.md

assertions.name: applies_webapp_heuristics
assertions.description: PM should apply Web Application heuristics: mention user flows/Given-When-Then, API contracts, test pyramid (unit+integration+e2e)

assertions.name: uses_correct_lifespan_labels
assertions.description: PM should label docs/project.md as [PERMANENT] and docs/state.md as [SESSION]

evals.name: concern-lifecycle-create-track-resolve
evals.prompt:
We just finished the payment-checkout feature. During implementation, we logged CONCERN-card-declined-handling.md (status: open). The concern says: 'Spec did not cover card_declined error. Applied safe default: show generic error, abort order. Resolution needed: product owner should specify retry behavior.'

Now that the feature is shipped, I've made a decision: we want to allow one retry with a different card, then hard-fail. Please resolve this concern properly.

<session_start_context>
project_root: /myapp
docs_exists: true
state_exists: true
project_exists: true
</session_start_context>

docs/state.md:
```
Phase: CLARIFY
Action: explore
Feature: none (payment-checkout shipped)
```

docs/concerns/CONCERN-card-declined-handling.md (status: open):
```
Status: open
Tier: 2
Feature: payment-checkout
Gap: Spec did not cover card_declined behavior
Safe default applied: show error, abort order
Resolution needed: Product owner to specify retry policy
```
evals.expected_output: PM should: (1) update CONCERN-card-declined-handling.md status from 'open' to 'resolved', (2) create a new decision file docs/decisions/YYYY-MM-DD-card-declined-retry-policy.md [PERMANENT] with Context/Decision/Consequences format, (3) NOT delete the concern file — concerns are [PERMANENT], (4) the decision file captures: the decision made (1 retry allowed, then hard fail), and the concern that prompted it

assertions.name: updates_concern_status_to_resolved
assertions.description: PM should update CONCERN-card-declined-handling.md status from 'open' to 'resolved'

assertions.name: creates_decision_file
assertions.description: PM should create docs/decisions/YYYY-MM-DD-card-declined-retry-policy.md with proper ADR format

assertions.name: decision_has_correct_format
assertions.description: Decision file should have: Date, Status: accepted, ## Context, ## Decision, ## Consequences sections

assertions.name: concern_file_kept_not_deleted
assertions.description: Concern file should be updated (status: resolved) but NOT deleted — it is [PERMANENT]

assertions.name: decision_references_concern
assertions.description: Decision file should reference the concern that prompted it (traceability)

evals.name: state-md-fidelity-after-wave
evals.prompt:
Wave 1 just completed for the user-authentication feature. Here's what happened:
- TASK-1 (User Registration): DONE — all tests pass, report saved
- TASK-2 (Login + JWT): DONE — all tests pass, report saved
- TASK-3 (JWT Middleware): DONE — all tests pass, report saved

Wave 2 tasks are: TASK-4 (Protected Routes), TASK-5 (Rate Limiting). These have not started yet.

Please update state.md to reflect this accurately.

<session_start_context>
project_root: /myapp
docs_exists: true
state_exists: true
project_exists: true
</session_start_context>

Current docs/state.md:
```
Phase: IMPLEMENT
Action: execute
Feature: user-authentication
Wave: 1/2
Tasks:
  [ ] TASK-1: User Registration
  [ ] TASK-2: Login + JWT
  [ ] TASK-3: JWT Middleware
  [ ] TASK-4: Protected Routes
  [ ] TASK-5: Rate Limiting
```
evals.expected_output: PM should produce updated state.md with: (1) Wave updated to 2/2 (moving to wave 2), (2) TASK-1/2/3 marked [x] done, (3) TASK-4/5 marked [ ] not started, (4) current Action updated to reflect wave 2 starting (execute wave 2), (5) worker report paths mentioned as docs/worker-reports/user-authentication/TASK-N.json [VERSION]

assertions.name: completed_tasks_marked_done
assertions.description: TASK-1, TASK-2, TASK-3 should be marked [x] or equivalent 'done' status

assertions.name: pending_tasks_still_open
assertions.description: TASK-4 and TASK-5 should remain [ ] not started

assertions.name: worker_report_paths_correct
assertions.description: Worker reports should be at docs/worker-reports/user-authentication/TASK-N.json with [VERSION] label

evals.name: version-release-memory-cleanup
evals.prompt:
The user-authentication feature is fully shipped and verified. We want to cut version v1.0 now. Please execute the Version Release Event.

<session_start_context>
project_root: /myapp
docs_exists: true
state_exists: true
project_exists: true
</session_start_context>

Current docs/ state:
- docs/project.md [PERMANENT] ✓
- docs/state.md [SESSION] — Phase: IMPLEMENT, all tasks done, verify passed
- docs/features/user-authentication/spec.md [PERMANENT] ✓
- docs/rules/constitution.md [PERMANENT] ✓
- docs/rules/coding-standards.md [PERMANENT] ✓
- docs/concerns/CONCERN-card-declined-handling.md [PERMANENT] — status: resolved
- docs/worker-reports/user-authentication/TASK-1.json [VERSION]
- docs/worker-reports/user-authentication/TASK-2.json [VERSION]
- docs/worker-reports/user-authentication/TASK-3.json [VERSION]
- docs/ROADMAP.md [VERSION] — contains: Phase 1 complete, Phase 2 planned for Q2
evals.expected_output: PM should execute Version Release Event: (1) read ROADMAP.md and summarize it, (2) create docs/versions/v1.0.md [PERMANENT] with archived ROADMAP content, (3) DELETE docs/worker-reports/ directory (all TASK-N.json files — they are [VERSION]), (4) CLEAR docs/ROADMAP.md entirely (reset to empty or remove), (5) KEEP all [PERMANENT] files untouched: project.md, features/, rules/, concerns/, decisions/

assertions.name: creates_version_archive
assertions.description: PM should create docs/versions/v1.0.md [PERMANENT] with archived content from ROADMAP

assertions.name: deletes_worker_reports
assertions.description: PM should DELETE docs/worker-reports/ (all [VERSION] files) — not keep them

assertions.name: clears_roadmap
assertions.description: PM should CLEAR docs/ROADMAP.md (reset to empty) — it is [VERSION]

assertions.name: keeps_permanent_files
assertions.description: PM should explicitly state that [PERMANENT] files are kept: project.md, features/, rules/, concerns/

assertions.name: correct_lifespan_logic
assertions.description: PM should correctly distinguish [VERSION] (delete) from [PERMANENT] (keep) and [SESSION] (reset)
